summary: I read both design guides (`backend-guide.md`, `ui-style-guide.md`) and the three Define-phase artifacts under review (requirements, discovery notes, open questions), then checked the specs against each relevant guide rule. Since this is the Define phase there is no code/diff — the work is business-level requirements plus discovery. The requirements are correctly technology-agnostic (no architectural choice that could deviate from a guide), and the discovery notes accurately carry the binding guide constraints forward: centralize dark mode rather than hand-roll per view (UI guide's explicit "Dark mode later" directive), preserve the semantic/reserved status colors and soft badge form with `status_helper.rb` as the single source (aligning R12/R13 with "status never by color alone"), route any server-side preference persistence through a PORO `.call`→Result service with a thin controller and Minitest per the backend guide, and honor the "propose a guide addition in the same PR" rule. Nothing contradicts a guide, so I emitted a **pass** verdict with no findings to `.pipeliner/phases/01-define/main/guide-alignment-critic/verdict.json`.
